title: citrus × aurantium var. bigaradia
intro: Citrus × aurantium var. bigaradia, also known as bitter orange, is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family and is a hybrid of the sour orange and the Seville orange. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve anxiety and stress, and lower blood pressure due to its high concentration of flavonoids and limonoids. The health benefits of bitter orange include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Bitter orange is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and essential oils, and is often combined with other herbs like peppermint and ginger to enhance its effects.
